context: Phase 9 v1 of delegate-to-ollama adds a per-user override mechanism. pick-model.sh sources ${DELEGATE_TO_OLLAMA_CONFIG:-$HOME/.claude/skills/delegate-to-ollama/config.sh} after the case statement populates `prefs`, allowing the override to reassign prefs per tier. init.sh emits a starter override to stdout based on `ollama list` output and the prefs parsed from pick-model.sh's case statement.

findings.title: Override path sources arbitrary bash via env var or $HOME
findings.details: pick-model.sh sources ${DELEGATE_TO_OLLAMA_CONFIG:-$HOME/.claude/skills/delegate-to-ollama/config.sh}. Whoever can write to that path or set DELEGATE_TO_OLLAMA_CONFIG executes arbitrary bash inheriting set -euo pipefail and the caller's environment. In a single-user dev context this is intentional, mirroring ~/.aiderrc and ~/.claude/settings.local.json. Risk increases sharply if pick-model.sh is ever invoked under sudo, in a CI runner with a shared HOME, or via a service account that doesn't fully control its HOME.

findings.title: DELEGATE_TO_OLLAMA_CONFIG env var has higher precedence than $HOME path
findings.details: Any process that can pre-set this env var redirects override sourcing without filesystem write access. A malicious shell function wrapping ollama, installed via a copy-pasted setup script, could set it to point at attacker content. Same overall threat model as F1 but easier to exploit in unguarded contexts.

findings.title: init.sh emits unescaped model names from ollama list into generated bash
findings.details: init.sh wraps each model name in double quotes via printf with no escape pass. If an Ollama model name ever contains a literal double-quote or dollar sign, the generated config.sh would have broken quoting or unintended expansion when sourced. Ollama's name grammar restricts these in practice but the validator is permissive.

findings.title: init.sh parses pick-model.sh's case block via perl regex without integrity check
findings.details: If pick-model.sh is ever modified by an attacker (e.g. via a compromised npx skills add install), init.sh trusts its content as the source of truth for prefs. Generated override files would propagate any malicious patterns into per-user configs. Threat model is supply-chain-via-skill-update, not direct exploitation.

findings.title: Override sourcing fails noisily under set -e propagation
findings.details: A user with a malformed override file gets an unhelpful exit code from pick-model.sh because set -euo pipefail propagates through the source. This is informational, not exploitable, but worsens UX of the personalisation feature.
